primary_sources.title: Johor State Legislative Assembly vote, 7 May 2026 (Hansard record): 40-16 in favour of constitutional amendment adding 5 appointed assemblymen
Johor State Legal Adviser's opinion, September 2025: amendment inconsistent with Federal Constitution
Federal Constitution Eighth Schedule §4(1) (description of state assemblies as having 'elected members')

amendment_text_change.after: 56 elected members plus up to 5 members appointed by the Sultan on Executive Council advice

notes: Johor's state assembly passed an amendment allowing up to five appointed members in addition to 56 elected ones. BN's 40-seat majority cleared the two-thirds bar (40 of 56). The state's own Legal Adviser had warned in September 2025 the amendment was inconsistent with the Federal Constitution. Pahang precedent (2024): five appointees added, all male, all party-aligned. Johor's pace (one day to enact and presumably appoint) compresses what Pahang took four years to do. The structural significance is the state-level constitutional-drift channel — see Mechanism §5.5 of the engine design doc. No federal court ruling yet on whether Eighth Schedule §4(1) actually bars appointed assemblymen.
